summary: Recent updates to WebRTC, the technology behind video calls, focused on improving how audio and video devices are managed and logged. Key changes include better memory management through the use of `MakeRefPtr`, which enhances the efficiency of audio device enumeration and source creation. Additionally, **the update to reject H.264 (a widely-used video compression standard) queries that exceed specified resolution and frame rate limits** helps ensure that only suitable media capabilities areused, providing a smoother user experience. These enhancements not only streamline functionality but also strengthen the overall reliability and performance of WebRTC in Firefox.
